q: The baby was talking to her toys. She thought they were animate. (Join the sentences, using 'as if / as though') 2024
a: The baby was talking to her toys as if they were animate.

q: My cousin looks excited and happy. I think he has got some good news. (Join the sentences, using 'as if / as though'.) 2024
a: My cousin looks excited and happy as if he has got some good news.

q: He runs and jumps like he has superpowers. (Use 'as if / as though'.) 2024
a: He runs and jumps as if he had superpowers.

q: The lion in the picture is lifelike. I felt it is about to roar. (Join the sentences, using 'as if / as though'.) 2024
a: The lion in the picture is lifelike as if it were about to roar.

q: He talks to me very loudly. He thinks I am a deaf person. (Join the sentences, using 'as if / as though'.) 2024
a: He talks to me very loudly as if I were a deaf person.

q: The gardener whispered a few words of encouragement to his plants. He thought they were alive. (Join the sentences, using 'as if'.) 2025
a: The gardener whispered a few words of encouragement to his plants as if they were alive.

q: Although she is not a professional singer, she performs like a music icon. (Join the sentences, using 'as if/ as though'.) 2025
a: She performs as if/as though she were a music icon although she is not a professional singer. (Although.... , .......)

q: The girl spoke to the teddy bear softly. She thought it was her best friend. (Use 'as if'.) 2025
a: The girl spoke to the teddy bear softly as if it were her best friend.

q: She was very excited. It seemed like she had won a prize. (Join the sentences, using 'as if'.) 2025
a: She was very excited as if she had won a prize.

q: A dog was barking at a delivery man. It thought he was a burglar. (Join the sentences, using 'as if/as though'.) 2026
a: A dog was barking at a delivery man as if it thought he was a burglar.

q: The weather is not that bad, but she dresses like it is freezing outside. (Join the sentences, using 'as if/ as though'.) 2025
a: She dresses as if it is freezing outside, (although the weather is not that bad).

q: He was really tired. It looked like he hadn't slept in days. (Join the sentences, using 'as if') 2025
a: He was really tired as if he hadn't slept in days.

q: My sister talks to her dog. She thinks the dog understands everything she says. (Join the sentences, using 'as if'.) 2026
a: My sister talks to her dog as if it understood everything she says.

q: Bo Ma was talking to her pet dog Shwe Toe. She thought it was her child. (Join the sentences, using 'as if'.) 2026
a: Bo Ma was talking to her pet dog Shwe Toe as if it were her child.

q: He sings with great confidence. He thinks he is a professional singer. (Join the sentences, using 'as if'.) 2026
a: He sings as if he were a professional singer.
